4.4. Testing volatility feedback in financial returns data

In this section, we develop the Bayesian test statistics for testing the presence of volatility feedback in a stochastic volatility model with leverage effect (SVI). A standard stochastic volatility (SV) model can be extended to the SVI. model by allowing for the so-called leverage effect via introducing correlation in the disturbance of outcome and log-volatility equations (moner et al., 2007; Vp., 2003). Squeuer et al., 2004). In the control of these lilitations, our simulation results show that our suggested test performs better than its non-robust counterpart and has good finite sample properties.
